summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held an abbreviated regular meeting before going into a lengthy executive session to interview police officer applicants. The board approved the payment of bills, passed a resolution requesting the Clermont County Commissioners vacate a portion of East Huntley Road, and acknowledged the road and safety services departments for their work during heavy snow. The executive session lasted approximately three hours, after which the meeting adjourned with no additional motions.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for February 10, 2021 through February 23, 2021
Approved Resolution R-004-2021 requesting Clermont County Board of County Commissioners vacate a portion of East Huntley Road
Adjourned into executive session at 7:11 PM per ORC 121.22 for appointment/employment/compensation of public employee and review of negotiations/bargaining sessions with public employees
Adjourned the regular meeting at 10:04 PM
action_items: Administrator Peet to forward Resolution R-004-2021 to the County Commissioners and handle the remaining steps for the East Huntley Road vacation process
notable_topics: Abbreviated meeting due to lengthy executive session for police officer interviews
Snow response and road department operations, including salt shortage resolved with help from City of Cincinnati
Resolution R-004-2021 to vacate portion of East Huntley Road (Dixon family property)
New swings installed at park with Ava's Army fundraiser support, dedication planned for April 10th
Executive session for interviewing and considering appointment of police officer applicants
notes: The majority of the meeting was spent in executive session (7:11 PM to 10:03 PM) interviewing police officer applicants. The board narrowed candidates down to three. COVID restrictions required a large room for the interviews. No motions were made following the executive session. Transcript quality is poor with many repeated automated text insertions.
title: BOT 2.23.2021
